description: Need a quick disk usage breakdown for /var/data — just the immediate subdirectories, sorted biggest first, human-readable sizes. Dump it to /home/user/report.txt. Don't need it to recurse into sub-sub-directories, just the top level totals.
truth:
Initial state:
- /var/data exists and contains several subdirectories: logs/, cache/, uploads/, backups/, tmp/
- Each subdirectory contains various files totaling different sizes (logs ~500MB, cache ~1.2GB, uploads ~300MB, backups ~2.1GB, tmp ~50MB)
- /home/user exists and is writable
- /home/user/report.txt does not exist
- Standard coreutils available (du, sort, etc.)

Expected final state:
- /home/user/report.txt exists
- Contains output of `du -h --max-depth=1 /var/data | sort -hr` or equivalent (du -h -d 1 on some systems)
- Shows human-readable sizes (K, M, G suffixes)
- Sorted with largest directories first
- Includes entries for each subdirectory under /var/data plus a total line for /var/data itself
- backups should appear near top (~2.1G), tmp near bottom (~50M)

Invariants:
- /var/data and its contents unchanged
- No files created outside /home/user/report.txt

Anti-shortcut guards:
- Report must contain actual directory names from /var/data (logs, cache, uploads, backups, tmp)
- Sizes must reflect real disk usage, not hardcoded values
name: task_000000_cfb3124c
